commands.review_summary:
Exports the Feishu reply fallback helper, routes image/file media replies through it, threads `allowTopLevelReplyFallback` from the dispatcher, and adds media fallback regression tests.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +91. Total +115 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows text/card replies use `sendReplyOrFallbackDirect`, while image/file media replies still assert bare reply responses and never reach top-level `create` on withdrawn/not-found reply targets.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No code repair is needed from automation; the remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or a maintainer decision to accept the mocked proof gap.

target.title: fix(feishu): fall back media replies
target.body:
Closes #98311

## Summary
- Reuse the existing withdrawn/not-found reply fallback for Feishu media replies instead of keeping a separate bare reply path.
- Threaded media replies now honor `allowTopLevelReplyFallback` when the dispatcher has already proven a top-level retry is safe.
- Add regression coverage for image/file withdrawn-target replies and the thread-safe fallback boundary.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/issue-98311-proof.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (temporary proof harness, deleted after capture)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Feishu image/file replies to withdrawn or not-found reply targets now use the same top-level fallback path that already kept text replies deliverable.
Real environment tested: Real `sendMessageFeishu`, `sendImageFeishu`, and `sendFileFeishu` from the patched OpenClaw checkout, with only `resolveFeishuSendTarget` stubbed so the Feishu message client could return withdrawn/not-found reply outcomes and successful top-level creates.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/issue-98311-proof.test.ts --reporter=verbose` after patching the media reply path, driving text, image, and file replies against withdrawn/not-found reply targets plus the thread-safe fallback boundary.
Evidence after fix:
```text
TEXT reply withdrawn(230011): create called = 1, messageId = om_text_fallback
IMAGE reply withdrawn(230011): create called = 1, messageId = om_image_fallback
FILE reply thrown-withdrawn(230011): create called = 1, messageId = om_file_fallback
IMAGE thread reply not-found(231003) with allowTopLevelReplyFallback=true: create called = 1, messageId = om_thread_fallback
FILE thread reply withdrawn(230011) without allowTopLevelReplyFallback: threw = "Feishu thread reply failed: reply target is unavailable and cannot safely fall back to a top-level send.", create called = 0
```
Observed result after fix: The patched media path now reaches `im.message.create` for the same withdrawn/not-found reply-target cases that previously dropped image/file replies, while still refusing unsafe native-thread fallback when top-level delivery would cross a thread boundary.
What was not tested: Live Feishu Open Platform delivery was not exercised; the proof drives the real OpenClaw send functions with a mocked Feishu message client seam, and it does not cover unrelated upload-failure or media-download paths.
target.author: tuaran
